div1.entryFree: Accommodate, (cf. Unaccommodated), to supply with conveniences: a soldier is better --d (Qq a.) than with a wife, H4B III, 2, 72 (where Shallow's and Bardolph's remarks prove that the word was not yet in daily use, but rather affected). the safer sense will ne'er a. his master thus, Lr. IV, 6, 81. --d by the place, Cymb. V, 3, 32 (favoured).
